steps.title: Understand what it means for a point to satisfy an equation
steps.content: If the point $(7, 11)$ satisfies the equation $y = f(x)$, then substituting $x = 7$ gives $y = 11$. For any point $(a, b)$ that satisfies $y = f(x)$, we have $f(a) = b$.

steps.title: Apply this relationship to find f(7)
steps.content: Since $(7, 11)$ satisfies $y = f(x)$, we substitute $a = 7$ and $b = 11$ into the relationship $f(a) = b$ to get $f(7) = 11$. That confirms B is correct.
